input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Views"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard: single page, shown directly in the flyout -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products: flyout entry with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings: single page in its own flyout entry -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to create an AppShell.xaml for a .NET MAUI app with a specific navigation structure. Let me provide a complete example.

    <!-- Settings: single page in its own flyout entry -->

        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"

            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the structure:

**AppShell.xaml** — Three `FlyoutItem` entries:
- **Dashboard** → single `ShellContent` (no tabs).
- **Products** → two `Tab` children ("Active" and "Archived") which render as a bottom tab bar when that flyout item is selected.
- **Settings** → single `ShellContent`.

**AppShell.xaml.cs** — Registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a programmatic route so you can navigate to it from either products list with:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"productdetails?id={product.Id}");
```

To receive the parameter in `ProductDetailsPage`, apply the query attribute:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(ProductId), "id")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public string ProductId { get; set; }
}
```

**Key points:**
- `ContentTemplate` with `DataTemplate` defers page creation until the user navigates there.
- Routes declared in XAML (`Route="dashboard"`) are for Shell's hierarchy; `Routing.RegisterRoute` is for pages *outside* the visual tree (like detail pages pushed onto the nav stack).
- Replace the `icon_*.png` values with your actual asset filenames in `Resources/Images`.
